question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 removes `update-source Loopback0` from its iBGP neighbor toward R4
(192.0.2.4). R2 now sources BGP OPEN from the physical interface IP
(192.0.0.9, the R2-R3 link). R4 receives OPEN from an IP that doesn't
match its configured neighbor (R4 has `neighbor 192.0.2.2`). R4 silently
rejects the connection; the session never reaches Established. PE-A
(R2) and PE-B (R4) stop exchanging iBGP prefixes.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), update-source Loopback0 was removed from the iBGP neighbor toward R4 (192.0.2.4), so R2 sources its BGP OPEN from the physical interface IP 192.0.0.9 instead of its Loopback0.
R4 is configured for neighbor 192.0.2.2 and rejects the connection arriving from an unexpected source address, so the iBGP session cannot be established and PE-A (R2) and PE-B (R4) stop exchanging prefixes.
Fix: restore update-source Loopback0 on R2's R4 neighbor.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.2.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R2, R4 (2 instances):
- R2: Changed state to NSR-Ready
- R4: Changed state to NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 100
neighbor 192.0.2.4
 no update-source

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 100
neighbor 192.0.2.4
 update-source Loopback0

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 22:12:39.367 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Down - Neighbor local address configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 22:12:39.376 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.2 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 22:12:39.370 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 22:12:39.378 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
